bead.title: INT-CC: Claude Code integration test — install + skill activation + bootstrap scenario
bead.description:
Create tests/workflows/claude-code/ harness that exercises live `claude -p` against the HELIX skill bundle inside a Docker container.

**Deliverables**:
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/run-scenarios.sh` — orchestrator that builds image, runs each scenario, captures output, exits nonzero on any failure
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/scenarios/install-verify.prompt` — "Confirm HELIX is installed: list the routing skill name and the artifact catalog activities"
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/scenarios/skill-list.prompt` — "List the HELIX routing modes you can route to. Be concise."
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/scenarios/bootstrap.prompt` — TP-014 SCN-01 prompt: "I want to build a TODO list app. Use HELIX to capture the framing artifacts."
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/expected/` — assertion-text snippets each scenario response must contain (e.g., "input", "frame", "align" mode names; vision/PRD file artifacts; etc.)
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/recordings/INT-CC.tape` — vhs script
- `tests/workflows/claude-code/recordings/INT-CC.gif` — generated recording (committed)

**Harness shape** (reuse `tests/install/claude-code/Dockerfile` as the base image):
1. `docker build -t helix-int-test:claude-code -f tests/install/claude-code/Dockerfile .`
2. For each scenario file: `docker run --rm -e ANTHROPIC_API_KEY -v $PWD:/workspace/helix:ro helix-int-test:claude-code bash -c "claude -p < /workspace/helix/tests/workflows/claude-code/scenarios/<name>.prompt"`
3. Grep response for expected text from `expected/<name>.expect`
4. vhs renders the orchestrator session

<context-digest>
<principles>Validate Your Work · Runtime Neutrality · Design for Simplicity</principles>
<concerns>installer-coverage | screencast-evidence | runtime-parity</concerns>
<practices>vhs records the Docker run · scenarios are SCN-01 (bootstrap) plus install-verify + skill-list · ANTHROPIC_API_KEY required at runtime · plugin install via marketplace path</practices>
<governing>docs/helix/03-test/test-plans/TP-014-helix-workflow-coverage.md SCN-01 (bootstrap) + SCN-06 (review) · docs/install/claude-code.md · existing tests/install/claude-code/ scaffolding</governing>
</context-digest>
bead.acceptance:
(Supersedes the "grep response for expected text" step in the description.)
1. `bash tests/workflows/claude-code/run-scenarios.sh` exits 0 with valid Claude Code credentials available — OAuth creds mounted into the container (~/.claude/.credentials.json + a minimal ~/.claude.json carrying hasCompletedOnboarding/userID/oauthAccount) OR ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set. Not a hard requirement on the env var.
2. Activation is asserted from `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (with `--bare` and `--allowed-tools "Skill(helix)"` / `--permission-mode` so it is not blocked by a prompt): the harness parses the event stream and requires a tool_use event identifying the `helix` Skill invocation for the bootstrap scenario. Free-text keyword matching of the response is NOT the gate.
3. Negative control: `bash tests/workflows/claude-code/run-scenarios.sh --no-skill` (skill not installed) exits NONZERO, proving the activation assertion discriminates.
4. tests/workflows/claude-code/evals/routing.jsonl exists (phrase -> expected helix mode, mirroring .claude/skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl) and is exercised by run-scenarios.sh.
5. Scenarios cover install-verify, skill activation, and SCN-01 bootstrap.
6. tests/workflows/claude-code/recordings/INT-CC.gif generated via vhs and committed. docs/install/claude-code.md links it as a screencast.
